Now let me check the preload.js to see what's exposed to the renderer.

Found the bug. `createConversation` is missing from both `preload.js` and `ipc-handlers.js`. In `ClaudeView.js:287`, `createNewConversation()` early-returns when `api.createConversation` is undefined — so no conversations ever get created, nothing gets saved, and the list stays empty.

input.old_string:
// Conversation management
  listConversations: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('list-conversations'),
  loadConversation: (filename) => ipcRenderer.invoke('load-conversation', filename),
  saveConversation: (conv) => ipcRenderer.invoke('save-conversation', conv),
  deleteConversation: (filename) => ipcRenderer.invoke('delete-conversation', filename),
  renameConversation: (filename, newName) => ipcRenderer.invoke('rename-conversation', filename, newName),

messages.text:
Two changes made:

1. **`preload.js`** — added `createConversation: (name) => ipcRenderer.invoke('create-conversation', name)` to the context bridge
2. **`ipc-handlers.js`** — imported `createConversation` from `ConversationService` and registered the `'create-conversation'` IPC handler

With these in place, `createNewConversation()` in `ClaudeView.js` will no longer bail out early, conversations will be created on disk, and `listConversations` will return them properly. Restart the Electron app to pick up the changes.
